locations.name: Capo d'Orlando Marina
locations.description: Modern full-service marina on the Tyrrhenian coast, 14 nautical miles south of the Aeolian Islands. Major departure base with 553 berths, accommodating vessels up to 50m. Comprehensive facilities including shipyard, fuel, water, electricity, shops, and restaurants.
locations.reviewSummary: Modern, full-service marina with excellent infrastructure. 14nm from Aeolian Islands, ideal departure point for the archipelago.

windProtection.description: Protected from northerly winds, moderately exposed to southern and western swells

locations.dataSource: Official website capodorlandomarina.it, Navily, Cruisers Wiki
locations.notes: 553 berths total. Accommodates boats up to 50m. 48 dry berths available (max 45m LOA, 4.5m draft). Features shipyard, shopping arcade, 24-hour security. Ideal starting point for Aeolian Island cruises.

locations.name: Porto di Levante
locations.description: Main port of Vulcano. Protected anchorage with ferry dock, mooring buoys, and access to hot springs and volcanic sulphur pools. Well-equipped with fuel, water, and electricity services.
locations.reviewSummary: Main port with good shelter and services. Access to thermal springs. Ferry connections to other islands.

windProtection.description: Protected from north and west winds. Exposed to southerly and easterly swells, particularly vulnerable to SE wind rotation.

locations.notes: Approximately 120 berths. Ferry terminal creates regular wake surge. Thermal springs and volcanic vents add character but require awareness. Peak season bookings essential.

locations.name: Porto di Ponente
locations.description: Beautiful bay on the west coast of Vulcano. Scenic anchorage with sandy bottom and excellent holding. Protected from easterly winds.
locations.reviewSummary: Scenic bay with good sand holding. Popular alternative to Porto di Levante.

windProtection.description: Well protected from easterly winds. Exposed to westerly swell and fetch.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. No services. Short dinghy ride to villages. Better shelter than Porto di Levante in settled conditions. Sand provides good holding.

locations.name: Gelso Bay
locations.description: Southern anchorage on Vulcano. Sandy bay with deep holding. Best in settled weather conditions due to exposure to Mediterranean swells.
locations.reviewSummary: Scenic southern anchorage, requires calm conditions

windProtection.description: Protected from northerly winds. Fully exposed to southern and southeasterly swells. Suitable only in calm conditions.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Remote and scenic. Deep sand bottom provides excellent holding. Only viable in flat-calm conditions. Day-visit only recommended.

locations.name: Vulcanello
locations.description: Anchorage to the north of Vulcano proper, near the volcanic islet. Sandy bottom with reasonable protection. Popular for day visits.
locations.reviewSummary: Small anchorage north of Vulcano, good for day stops

windProtection.description: Protected from southerly winds. Exposed to strong northerly and northwesterly winds.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Day-visit recommended. Dramatic volcanic scenery. Good for exploring Vulcano's northern coast.

locations.name: Marina Lunga
locations.description: Principal port and marina of Lipari. Main ferry dock with extensive yacht pontoons, fuel station, water, electricity. Accommodates large vessels.
locations.reviewSummary: Main marina with comprehensive services. Ferry connections. Good shelter. Reliable services.

windProtection.description: Protected from northeasterly and easterly winds. Exposed to southwesterly and westerly swells. Ferry traffic area.

locations.notes: Also known as Porto Pignataro. Main infrastructure hub of Lipari. Busy with ferry traffic. Reserve in advance during summer months.

locations.name: Marina Corta
locations.description: Small historic harbor in Lipari town center. Attractive waterfront location with restaurants and shops. Limited draft and berth space.
locations.reviewSummary: Charming town center harbor, limited space, shallow

windProtection.description: Protected from northerly winds. Exposed to southwesterly and westerly swells.

locations.notes: Limited berths (approx 30-40). Maximum draft ~3m. Ideal for exploring Lipari town. Atmosphere and location compensate for limited size.

locations.name: Porto Pignataro (Private Marina)
locations.description: Private marina facility with modern pontoons and full services. Separate from main Marina Lunga operations. Deep water berths.
locations.reviewSummary: Private marina with good facilities, higher fees

windProtection.description: Similar protection to Marina Lunga. Protected from northerly winds, exposed to southwesterly swells.

locations.notes: Private facility with premium services. Deeper water than Marina Corta. Higher fees reflect quality of facilities. Good for extended stays.

locations.name: Lipari Sotto Monastero
locations.description: Anchorage area below the historic monastery. Protected bay with good holding. Scenic location with access to town.
locations.reviewSummary: Scenic anchorage near monastery, good holding

windProtection.description: Protected from northeasterly winds. Exposed to southwesterly swells.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Close to Lipari town center. Good for overnight stays. Historic setting.

locations.name: Punta Crepazza
locations.description: Southern anchorage on Lipari. Sandy bottom with excellent holding. Protected from northerly winds. Less crowded than northern anchorages.
locations.reviewSummary: South Lipari anchorage with good sand holding, quiet

windProtection.description: Good protection from north wind. Exposed to southerly and southeasterly swells.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Sandy holding is excellent. Quieter than northern Lipari. Dinghy ride to villages.

locations.name: Acquacalda
locations.description: Northwest coast anchorage on Lipari. Protected from southerly winds. Sandy bottom with reasonable holding.
locations.reviewSummary: NW Lipari anchorage, protected from south wind

windProtection.description: Protected from southerly winds. Exposed to northerly and northwesterly wind and swell.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Good for day stops. Useful for avoiding south wind effects when anchored elsewhere on Lipari.

locations.name: Canneto Bay
locations.description: East coast anchorage on Lipari near Canneto village. Protected from westerly winds. Popular with cruisers.
locations.reviewSummary: East coast bay, protected from west wind, good village access

windProtection.description: Protected from westerly winds. Exposed to northeasterly and easterly winds and swell.

locations.notes: Free or low-cost anchorage. Small village with provisions and restaurants accessible by dinghy. Good for relaxed stays.

locations.name: Valle Muria
locations.description: Southwest anchorage on Lipari. Scenic location with dramatic cliffs. Best in settled weather conditions.
locations.reviewSummary: Scenic SW Lipari anchorage, requires calm conditions

windProtection.description: Protected from easterly winds only. Fully exposed to westerly and southwesterly winds and swell.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Dramatic scenery with high cliffs. Day-visit only in calm conditions. Not recommended for overnight in summer months when SW wind occurs.

locations.name: Santa Marina Salina
locations.description: Main port of Salina. Best-equipped marina in the Aeolians after Lipari. Modern breakwater with stern-to berths, full services.
locations.reviewSummary: Excellent modern marina, well-protected, comprehensive services. Best in Aeolians after Lipari.

windProtection.description: Well protected from westerly winds. Exposed to northeasterly winds but reasonable shelter from swell.

locations.notes: Modern infrastructure with laid moorings on pontoons. Fuel station on outer breakwater. Water and electricity on pontoons. Secondary ferry port. Popular stopover.

locations.name: Rinella
locations.description: Small port on southwest Salina. Ferry port with limited berth space. Protected anchorage with sandy bottom.
locations.reviewSummary: Small SW Salina port, ferry terminal, limited space

windProtection.description: Protected from northeasterly and easterly winds. Exposed to southwesterly winds and swell.

locations.notes: Secondary ferry port. Limited anchoring space but less crowded than Santa Marina. Ferry traffic creates wake surge.

locations.name: Pollara Bay
locations.description: Spectacular northern anchorage with dramatic cliffs. Deep water and scenic location. Requires settled weather.
locations.reviewSummary: Spectacular dramatic cliffs, settled weather only, deep water

windProtection.description: Exposed to northerly winds and swells. Spectacular but vulnerable to wind shifts.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Iconic Pollara cliffs offer spectacular scenery. Day-visit only. Deep water limits anchoring options. Must leave immediately if wind shifts.

locations.name: Lingua
locations.description: Quiet southeast anchorage on Salina. Sandy bottom with good holding. Protected from westerly winds.
locations.reviewSummary: Quiet SE Salina anchorage, good sand, good shelter from west

windProtection.description: Good protection from westerly winds. Exposed to easterly and northeasterly winds.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Quiet and scenic. Sandy holding is excellent. Short dinghy ride to village. Good for relaxed overnight.

locations.name: Malfa
locations.description: North coast anchorage on Salina. Exposed to northerly winds but scenic mountain backdrop.
locations.reviewSummary: N coast Salina, exposed to north wind, scenic

windProtection.description: Protected from southerly winds only. Fully exposed to northerly and northeasterly winds.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Best as a day stop or in southerly wind conditions. North wind exposure makes it less reliable for overnight.

locations.name: San Pietro (Panarea Buoy Field)
locations.description: Protected mooring buoy field with 31 buoys. Primary anchorage for Panarea. Well protected from westerly winds.
locations.reviewSummary: Excellent buoy field with good protection and good holding. Well-maintained moorings.

windProtection.description: Protected from westerly winds. Exposed to easterly and northeasterly winds but reasonable overall shelter.

locations.dataSource: Navily, sailing guides, buoy field operators
locations.notes: 31 maintained mooring buoys. Ferry jetty to north of buoy field (100m). Well-protected anchorage. Popular and often full in summer.

locations.name: Cala Junco
locations.description: Scenic bay on Panarea with sand and gravel bottom. Popular with cruisers for its beauty and shelter.
locations.reviewSummary: Beautiful scenic bay with good holding, popular anchorage

windProtection.description: Protected from northerly winds. Exposed to southerly winds but good overall protection in normal summer conditions.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. One of the most beautiful anchorages in the Aeolians. Sand and gravel holding is excellent. Short dinghy to village.

locations.name: Cala Zimmari
locations.description: Small beach anchorage on Panarea. Sandy bottom. Close to village.
locations.reviewSummary: Small beach anchorage, easy dinghy access to village

windProtection.description: Protected from northwesterly winds. Exposed to southeasterly winds.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Very close to village. Good beach landing. Limited anchoring space.

locations.name: Dattilo/Basiluzzo Area
locations.description: Anchorage near small volcanic islets. Scenic location. Best in settled weather conditions.
locations.reviewSummary: Near islets, scenic, settled weather recommended

windProtection.description: Limited protection. Exposed to most wind directions. Requires calm conditions.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Dramatic scenery with volcanic islets. Day-visit only. Settled weather essential. Deep water and limited holding.

locations.name: Scari
locations.description: Main landing on Stromboli. Small jetty with limited facilities. Only town with services.
locations.reviewSummary: Only main landing, exposed, limited services, working anchorage

windProtection.description: Limited protection. Exposed to northerly and easterly winds. Typically exposed anchorage.

locations.notes: Only access to main Stromboli town. Exposed anchorage used as a working port. Ferry traffic. Dramatic volcanic backdrop. Day-visit recommended unless calm conditions.

locations.name: Ginostra
locations.description: Tiny port on west coast. Smallest port in Italy. Very limited infrastructure. Picturesque village.
locations.reviewSummary: Smallest port in Italy, picturesque, very limited, sheltered from east

windProtection.description: Protected from easterly winds. Exposed to westerly and southwesterly winds and swell.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Charming historic village reachable only by boat or mule. Very limited space. Settled weather essential. No electricity or water connections.

locations.name: Punta Lena
locations.description: Northeast anchorage on Stromboli. Sandy and stony bottom. Protected from southwesterly winds.
locations.reviewSummary: NE Stromboli anchorage, sand/stone, protected from SW

windProtection.description: Protected from southwesterly winds. Exposed to northerly and northwesterly winds.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Day-visit recommended. Good shelter from southwest wind. Remote location.

locations.name: Strombolicchio (Rock)
locations.description: Dramatic anchorage near Strombolicchio rock. Scenic but challenging. Day stop only.
locations.reviewSummary: Dramatic rock formation, day stop only, scenic

windProtection.description: Limited protection. Exposed to most wind directions except southerly.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Dramatic scenic rock visible from distance. Day-visit only. Poor holding. Exposed location requires calm conditions.

locations.name: Filicudi Porto
locations.description: Main port of Filicudi. Small jetty with limited berth space. Protected from southwesterly winds.
locations.reviewSummary: Small main port, SW protected, limited space

windProtection.description: Protected from southwesterly winds only. Exposed to most other directions.

locations.notes: Small port with limited berths. Ferry access. Limited services but village accessible by dinghy. Good for overnight in summer.

locations.name: Pecorini a Mare (Buoy Field)
locations.description: Small fishing village with available mooring buoys. Protected anchorage area.
locations.reviewSummary: Quiet village anchorage with buoys, good holding

windProtection.description: Protected from westerly winds. Exposed to easterly winds.

locations.notes: Mooring buoys available. Quiet fishing village. Good sand holding. Remote location with limited services but accessible village.

locations.name: Capo Graziano
locations.description: Southeast anchorage on Filicudi. Protected from westerly and northwesterly winds. Sandy bottom.
locations.reviewSummary: SE Filicudi, protected from W/NW, sandy bottom

windProtection.description: Protected from westerly and northwesterly winds. Exposed to easterly and southeasterly winds.

locations.notes: Free anchorage. Good sand holding. Remote. Dinghy access to nearby villages.

locations.name: Alicudi Porto (Buoy Field)
locations.description: Small village anchorage with buoys available. Most remote of the Aeolian Islands. Very limited services.
locations.reviewSummary: Remote island, buoys available, very limited services, deep water

windProtection.description: Fully exposed. No natural protection. Suitable only in extremely calm conditions.

locations.notes: Mooring buoys available. Most remote Aeolian island. Very limited infrastructure. No services. Settled weather essential. Day-visit only recommended.
